title: iris spuria subsp. notha
intro: Iris spuria subsp. notha is a lesser-known subspecies of the iris plant, scientifically classified as a type of flowering plant in the Iridaceae family. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Iris spuria subsp. notha include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and alleviating symptoms of respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its health benefits, including soothing digestive issues and promoting overall well-being.
